member.name: T:CS3500.Networking.NetworkConnection
member.summary: Wraps the StreamReader/writer/TcpClient together so we don't have to keep creating all three for network actions.

member.name: F:CS3500.Networking.NetworkConnection._tcpClient
member.summary: The true networking object.

member.name: F:CS3500.Networking.NetworkConnection._reader
member.summary: Ability to read from the tcpClient.

member.name: F:CS3500.Networking.NetworkConnection._writer
member.summary: Ability to write to the tcpClient.

member.name: M:CS3500.Networking.NetworkConnection.#ctor(System.Net.Sockets.TcpClient)
member.summary:
Initializes a new instance of the class.
Create a network connection object.

member.name: M:CS3500.Networking.NetworkConnection.#ctor
member.summary:
Initializes a new instance of the class.
Create a network connection object. The tcpClient will be unconnected at the start.

member.name: P:CS3500.Networking.NetworkConnection.IsConnected
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the socket is connected.

member.name: M:CS3500.Networking.NetworkConnection.Connect(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Try to connect to the given host:port. Allow users to cancel if they want by using the cancellationToken.
member.param: The URL or IP address, e.g., www.cs.utah.edu, or 123.201.51.5.

member.name: M:CS3500.Networking.NetworkConnection.Send(System.String)
member.summary: Send a message to the remote server. If the contains new lines, these will be treated on the receiving side as multiple messages. This method should attach a newline to the end of the Your should normally not contain an ending newline.
member.param: The string of characters to send.

member.name: M:CS3500.Networking.NetworkConnection.ReadLine
member.summary: Read a message from the other side of the socket. The message will contain all characters up to the first new line. See .

member.name: M:CS3500.Networking.NetworkConnection.Disconnect
member.summary: If connected, disconnect and clean up.

member.name: M:CS3500.Networking.NetworkConnection.Dispose
member.summary: Automatically called with using statement.

member.name: T:CS3500.Networking.Server
member.summary: Represents a server task that waits for connections on a given port and calls the delegate when a connection is made.

member.name: M:CS3500.Networking.Server.StartServer(System.Action{CS3500.Networking.NetworkConnection},System.Int32)
member.summary: Wait on a TcpListener for new connections. "Alert" the main program via a callback mechanism.
member.param: Handler for what the program wants to do when a connection is made. This should be run asynchronously via a new thread.

member.name: M:CS3500.SnakeGame.NetworkController.FinishGame
member.summary: Updates the end timestamp for the db entry for this game Also closes the network connection with the game server

member.name: M:CS3500.SnakeGame.NetworkController.AddGameToDB
member.summary: Adds a new row to the Games table in the database and gets this game's ID

member.name: M:CS3500.SnakeGame.NetworkController.UpdateScore(CS3500.SnakeGame.Snake)
member.summary: Updates the max score in the database for the given snake in this game

member.name: M:CS3500.SnakeGame.NetworkController.DisconnectSnake(CS3500.SnakeGame.Snake)
member.summary: Updates the leave time in the database for the given snake in this game

member.name: M:CS3500.SnakeGame.NetworkController.AddSnake(CS3500.SnakeGame.Snake)
member.summary: Adds a new snake to the database for this game

member.name: T:CS3500.SnakeGame.Apple
member.summary: A class to represent a wall section

member.name: M:CS3500.SnakeGame.Apple.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: T:CS3500.SnakeGame.Snake
member.summary: FIXME.nsake

member.name: P:CS3500.SnakeGame.Snake.Disconnect
member.summary: Disconnect property

member.name: P:CS3500.SnakeGame.Snake.Join
member.summary: True first time

member.name: P:CS3500.SnakeGame.Snake._bodyXF
member.summary: Hack for using floating point snakes from server. hoepfully will be fixed soon.

member.name: P:CS3500.SnakeGame.Snake._body
member.summary: The snake's body, where the first point is the head and the last is the tail.

member.name: F:CS3500.SnakeGame.Snake.down
member.summary: Down direction.

member.name: F:CS3500.SnakeGame.Snake.up
member.summary: Up direction.

member.name: F:CS3500.SnakeGame.Snake.right
member.summary: Right direction.

member.name: F:CS3500.SnakeGame.Snake.left
member.summary: Left direction.

member.name: M:CS3500.SnakeGame.Snake.#ctor(CS3500.SnakeGame.XY,CS3500.SnakeGame.XY)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:CS3500.SnakeGame.Snake.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class. from 0,0 to 1,1.

member.name: P:CS3500.SnakeGame.Snake.Head
member.summary: Gets or sets the front of the snake.

member.name: P:CS3500.SnakeGame.Snake.Tail
member.summary: Gets or sets the final segment of the snake.

member.name: P:CS3500.SnakeGame.Snake.Shoulder
member.summary: Gets the second segment of the snake (the one directly before the head), i.e., the shoulder.

member.name: P:CS3500.SnakeGame.Snake.Hips
member.summary: Gets the second to last segment of the snake (the one directly after the the tail), i.e., the hips.

member.name: P:CS3500.SnakeGame.Snake.Direction
member.summary: Gets the current direction of the snake, which is the vector from the second segment (shoulder) to the head.
member.remarks: The direction is the normalized difference between the head and the shoulder (second segment).

member.name: P:CS3500.SnakeGame.Snake.TailDirection
member.summary: Gets the current direction of the TAIL segment of the snake, which is the vector from the tail to the next element.

member.name: P:CS3500.SnakeGame.Snake.Body
member.summary: Gets the points in the snake.
member.returns: The list of points in the snake.

member.name: M:CS3500.SnakeGame.Snake.ToString
member.summary: A simple representation for debugging.
member.returns: A string representation of the snake.

member.name: M:CS3500.SnakeGame.Snake.MoveForward
member.summary: Moves the snake forward by one unit in the current direction.
member.remarks: The movement involves calculating the new position of the head and adjusting the rest of the body accordingly. The tail moves to follow the body segments.

member.name: M:CS3500.SnakeGame.Snake.ChangeDirection(System.String)
member.summary: FIXME.

member.name: T:CS3500.SnakeGame.Wall
member.summary: A class to represent a wall section

member.name: M:CS3500.SnakeGame.Wall.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:CS3500.SnakeGame.Wall.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:CS3500.SnakeGame.Wall.Init
member.summary: Since the JSON deserializer uses the default constructor, then sets values directly, there's no good way to ensure min/max x/y get set. A better solution would be to make p1 and p2 properties, and update these values in the p1/p2 setter

member.name: T:CS3500.SnakeGame.World
member.summary: Represents the game world that contains snakes, apples, and walls.

member.name: M:CS3500.SnakeGame.World.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class. Sets up the world with boundaries and random apples.

member.summary: FIXME.

member.summary: FIXME.

member.summary: FIXME.
